title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: More than 60 international tall ships and military vessels arrived in Boston for Sail Boston 250's six-day event.
The USS Constitution led the parade of ships representing over 20 countries into Boston Harbor.
Thousands of visitors gathered to witness the final stop of the Sail250 tour celebrating Boston's maritime history.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox selected shortstop Jake Schaffner with the No. 20 pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, from UNC, was ranked 75th by MLB.com, standing 6'2", 200 pounds, and bats left-handed.
He recorded a .356 batting average, 26 steals, and a .987 fielding percentage in 68 games at shortstop.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Sail Boston Grand Parade of Sail 2026 featured historic ships, including the USS Constitution, along Boston's waterfront.
The event marks a chance to appreciate maritime history, with ships like the replica schooner Bluenose Two from Nova Scotia participating.
Organizers highlight the event's multi-generational appeal and its role in showcasing Boston's international maritime connections.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello awarded 'Cy Yuk' by The Athletic for posting a 10.35 ERA in eight starts for the Red Sox.
Bello, demoted to Triple-A, faced difficulties with a 2.21 WHIP and allowed opponents a .365 average.
Despite struggling as a starter, Bello excelled in relief for Boston with a 0.71 ERA over 25.1 innings.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Boston Red Sox are on a seven-game winning streak, close to an AL wild-card spot.
Red Sox face a decision on whether to buy or sell players before the 2026 MLB trade deadline.
General Manager Craig Breslow aims to show the team's competitiveness amid varying reports on his job status.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira returned to music and concert tours after a seven-year hiatus, performing at Boston's TD Garden.
Her setlist included favorites like 'Hips Don't Lie,' 'Whenever, Wherever,' and new tracks from 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran'.
The performance highlighted Shakira's resilience, featuring elaborate stage setups and themes of personal growth and empowerment.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Healey-Driscoll administration awards $18.6 million in tax credits for 662 new homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects involve converting historic buildings and mills into apartments in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester.
Massachusetts Gateway Cities benefit from boosted tax credits to address high housing costs and housing shortages state-wide.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested by a SWAT team in Albany, NY, for two armed bank robberies in June.
$6,000 was stolen from St. Mary's Bank in Nashua, NH, and $12,000 from Chase Bank in Boonton, NJ.
The arrest was part of 'Operation Summer Heat 2.0,' targeting violent crime, involving multiple FBI and police teams.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, from Berlin, NH, was found dead near Route 2 in Shelburne after being missing since July 4.
Grondin was last seen riding his Harley-Davidson; his body and motorcycle were discovered by a local resident.
Preliminary findings suggest Grondin crossed lanes and struck a post; no other vehicle is believed to be involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declared July 11, 2026, as 'Noah Kahan Day' in Massachusetts.
Noah Kahan is the first artist to headline four consecutive sold-out shows at Fenway Park.
Kahan is recognized for his music and mental health advocacy through the Busyhead Project.
